brief_justification: Agent chose C over the correct B — plausible but wrong, since B's 'overstates strong evidence' precisely captures the situation; the response traces neuroimaging logic through activation→correlation→localization→distributed-processing across multiple levels; explicitly names the fallacy type, addresses why each option was rejected, and flags the ontological category error; applies counterfactual check (what claim would justify B?) and boundary test (what would activation alone support?); carefully distinguishes what evidence establishes from what the claim asserts, including noting what would shift the classification; systematically evaluates all four options with specific rejection criteria for each; every inference step is made explicit and traceable, including the activation-to-necessity gap and the replication-to-ontology distinction.
